texts.full_text: milk. Infant exposure to lead through breast milk is of special concern because breast milk is considered the best food source for infants under 6 months. In this study, a total of the mothers provided colostrum samples once in the early postpartum period, but only 16 of them provided breast milk weekly at 1-60 days postpartum. The geometric mean of lead concentrations in all colostrum samples (n=72) was 7.68+/-8.24 microg/L. The concentration of lead in the breast milk of the consumption group (the mothers who consumed traditional Chinese herbs) was 8.59+/-10.95 microg/L, a level 3-fold higher than that observed for non-consumers (i.e., no herb use). A significant positive correlation between maternal and infant lead concentrations in breast milk is found at the p< or = .024 statistical significance levels through linear regression analysis using Pearson's correlations. For some mothers who consumed traditional Chinese herbs, a significant decrease of concentration was observed during 3-8 months postpartum. Full Text URL: Dietary exposure; Environmental health monitoring/methods ; Infant mortality/ statistics

texts.full_text: is oftentimes necessary to determine the timing of the edges of different signals relative to one another. If a pulse is generated with an edge that must have a predefined timing, which is to say a predetermined time relationship (for example, a delay) relative to a rising or falling edge 1 of a control signal, it is often required that this timing be precisely defined. However, due to variations in the electrical and physical properties of the components used, for example, this timing is not always reproducible, even when identical circuit layouts are used, so that the edge 2 of the pulse, which must have a predefined timing, will always occur at an offset relative to edge 1, which can only be guaranteed within a tolerance range. This type of timing is often used, for example, in read-only memories (ROM) for selecting a word line from the address. If, for example, a plurality of word lines are selected and the same address is decoded in parallel, there are several possibilities for constructing the decoding circuits. However, they cannot be constructed in such a way that only the first address input signal has an effect on the selection of the first word line, while the second and further address input signals must arrive later than the first one. However, if the edge 2 of the decoding pulse has an offset in relation to edge 1 of the address input, a delay will be created that has an effect on the timing of the selection of the word line relative to the clock. The offset of the timing of edge 2 relative to edge 1 must be taken into account when designing

Heather Knight, who also has a Ph.D. from the Robotics Institute at Carnegie Mellon University wrote and published an essay regarding a post-doctoral research she made with a colleague from Stanford University on social robotics. The subject of their study was the accuracy of Tesla's autopilot technology when it comes to human-robot interaction. To test this, they drove around on the streets of Southern California and came up with the conclusion that although Tesla auto-pilot responded well to cars, it did not give the same love to cyclists leading them to advise never to put the car on auto-ppilot mode in areas where there are many bicyclists. Environmental Impact: Like many chemicals found around us today, they can accumulate over time in the environment posing risks to aquatic life once washed down drains or handled improperly during manufacturing processes. As I pondered this analogy about food labeling trends and cosmetic ingredient safety while catching up on my reading at Sprout's shoot last week (amidst some coconut-oil-induced shaving mishaps), it struck me how important education is. We need to foster understanding because, just like the hazy comprehension around parabens or trending diets marked with "organic" labels without verification—it’s essential we grasp what these terms mean. It empowers us for more informed decisions rather than jumping on trends influenced by appealing marketing campaigns alone. Thus begins my mission: diving deeper into deciphering beauty and food jargons, one term at a time—encouraging others to do the same as they navigate through aisles laden with shiny promises but potentially empty value propositions. In essence: - Embrace curiosity. - Seek out reputable sources for information. This means not just relying on internet search results or word-of-mouth

texts.full_text: able to successfully post the http request. I cannot disconnect the docker container from my user-defined network. I also cannot add my webserver to that network, as it is not running in a container. Also, I know it is trivial to connect the other way (curl to a webserver running in a docker container) but this is not my use case. How can I successfully route that http request from the docker container which is part of a user defined network to my localhost webserver? A: You can do this with the actual IP address of your local computer. So for example, if your en0 IP is 10.100.20.32 on your host OS, you can run: docker exec testcontainer curl --data "foobaz=foo" http://10.100.20.32:9000/ which will successfully allow you to make the http requests. Note that if you are doing this from a container on the host docker network, this is trivial, as you can directly access localhost or 0.0.0.0 without having to use the actual machine

Overcoming all but one major rally since then to reach our current levels near $20/ounce today (March) . But what does history say? Do the price fluctuations seen in silver recently suggest anything about its future performance? The first thing we must consider is how far back do you want me go and remember - no two events will ever be exactly alike. Silver futures were listed on COMEX for trading by December 1974. That�s a pretty good starting point right there; that was the beginning of major fluctuations in precious metals prices, especially silver's performance since it tends to have more volatility than gold . If you want something farther back - then we will be looking at pre-WWII era trends. You may recall that there were some large swings in silver prices early this past year; remember all those margin increases which happened about every week? texts.full_text: y of Israel, was recentlyinterviewed by Ehud Banai about her work with Talmudic stories. In this interview conducted in Tel Aviv's Beit Midrash Elul library on the eve before Rosh Hashanah 2018, Ruth Calderon shared insights into how storytelling and education intersect within Jewish culture. Ehud: You have been working extensively for many years with Talmudic stories. What is your personal connection to these texts? RuthCalderon (RC): My journey began in the early days of my academic career when I realized that there was a gap between how we teach and study traditional Jewish literature, such as our Tanakh or Mishnah Torah – which are rich with stories. The Talmud itself is filled not onlywith laws but also parables. Ehud: How do you think the use of storytelling can be beneficial in today's educational landscape? RC (Ruth): Storytelling has always been a powerful tool for education, and it becomes even more crucial when we're trying to connect with students who may feel alienated by traditional religious texts. By telling stories from these ancient sources creatively – adapting them into contemporary settings or using modern language without losing the essence of their message - they become accessible. Ehud: How did you first get involved in this area, and what inspired your work? RC (Ruth): My interest was sparked by a desire to bridge gaps between generations. I saw that many younger people were not engaging with traditional texts due possibly because these stories are perceived as archaic or irrelevant today – especially when they're presented without the cultural context. Ehud: What has been your most rewarding experience in this field? RC (Ruth): One of my proudest moments was witnessing a young person who had never shown interest before, become deeply moved by one story. It made me realize that even seemingly disengaged individuals can be profoundly touched when they're given the chance to see themselves reflected through these timele

texts.full_text: for Academic Researchers By Dian Schaffhauser 01/20/16 Academic researchers now have free access to a sizable new dataset for the purposes of expanding the scientific world's understanding of Web sciences. Yahoo Labs released the "Yahoo News Recommendation" dataset, which consists of data on 110 billion events, taking up 13.5 terabytes in its uncompressed format. The information inside the dataset is completely anonymized and maintained in the Yahoo Labs Webscope data-sharing program, the company reported. It's made up of user content interactions for about 20 million users during the period from January to December 2015 on Yahoo News sites across several countries. The dataset includes a variety of actions such as clicks, likes, shares, and comments on news articles, along with metadata like article categories, publication times, and user demographics (in an aggregated form). Researchers can use this data for various purposes including improving recommendation algorithms, understanding information diffusion patterns, analyzing social media impact on news consumption, and enhancing personalization techniques. Yahoo emphasized that the dataset adheres to strict privacy standards. It has been stripped of any personally identifiable information (PII) to ensure user anonymity while still providing valuable insights into how users interact with online content. To access this resource, researchers must apply through Yahoo's Webscope program, which reviews applications for academic and non-commercial research purposes only. This ensures that the data is used responsibly and ethically by qualified individuals in the scientific community. The release of such a large-scale dataset

texts.full_text: healthcare infrastructure challenges, and logistical barriers [@pone.0049928-Williams1]. Consequently, despite significant efforts to expand access to ART and implement the 'three I's strategy', the burden of HIV-associated TB remains disproportionately high in this region [@pone.0049928-Corbett1]. The effectiveness of these interventions is further compounded by issues such as drug resistance, which can undermine treatment outcomes and complicate efforts to control the spread of TB [@pone.0049928-Dawson1]. Additionally, social and structural factors, including stigma, discrimination, and poverty, contribute to the barriers faced by individuals in accessing and adhering to treatment [@pone.0049928-Barnighausen1]. This paper aims to review the current landscape of HIV-associated TB, focusing on the effectiveness of the recommended programme interventions and the challenges that hinder their implementation. We will also explore innovative strategies and approaches that may help to overcome these challenges and improve outcomes for individuals affected by HIV-associated TB

texts.full_text: of poly(ADP-ribose) is produced. However in nonturnover conditions only low to moderate level was detected on histones suggesting some other process could be responsible for this turnover system. The major protein acceptors are H1 (the linker-histone), the core component and several basic regions, which include Lys9 of nucleosome octamer as well. In all systems examined here a high proportion poly(ADP-ribosyl)ation was found on histones in comparison to other chromatin components suchas DNA or RNA suggesting that this process is highly selective. The results show the importance for turnover conditions, since only under medium and fast turnovers were significant levels of automodification detected. In nonturnover systems low amounts (0-10%) poly(ADPribosyl)ation was found on histones indicating a possible role in chromatin structure or function which is independent from nucleotide excision repair. In conclusion, it appears that turnover conditions play an important part for the amount and selectivity of automodification. It can be assumed therefore this process could have some functional significance under physiological as well pathological condition suchas during cell division when high levels are found on histones or after DNA damage where both poly(ADP-ribose)polymerase activity is increased in cells. texts.full_text: of the Invention The present invention relates to a method an apparatus for automatically detecting breast tumors and lesions in images. More specifically, the present invention relates to a method and apparatus for automatically segmenting, classifying, and detecting breast lesions in ultrasound, digital and analog mammogram, and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) images. 2. Related Art Breast cancer is the most frequently diagnosedmalignancy and the second leading cause of mortality in women. In the last decade, ultrasound imaging, along with digital mammography, has become the standard for breast cancer diagnosis. Mammography is the most effective method for earlydetection of breast cancer, and periodic screening of asymptomatic women reduces the mortality rate. Typically, the first step in breast cancer detection is a screening mammogram, which comprises a low-dose x-ray examination on asymptomatic women. This can be followed by adiagnostic mammogram, which is an x-ray examination done to evaluate a breast complaint or to investigate an abnormality found during a physical exThe invention described focuses on enhancing the automatic detection of breast tumors and lesions through advanced imaging techniques such as ultrasound, digital/mammographic analog mammograms, and MRI. Here's astructured summary: 1.Objective: - The primary goal is to develop an automated method for segmenting (identifying), classifying, and detecting breast lesions in various medical images. 2. Context: - Breast cancer ranks as the most common malignancy among women with significant mortality rates; early detection through imaging plays acrucial role. 3. Invention Focus: - Utilizes technology to improve accuracy and efficiency of lesion identification across different modalities, potentially reducing human error. 4. Technical Aspects: - Likely employs software algorithms (possibly AI-driven) for image analysis without requiring new hardware integration with existing systems. 5. Benefits: - Enhances early detection rates by improvingdiagnostic consistency; particularly beneficial in resource-limited settings where radiologist availabilityis low.

texts.full_text: end starting with Hume's view, which you can read about here or here, followed by Wittgenstein on rule-following, which you can read about here. Of course, if you are more familiar with classical literature, you can check out Sextus Empiricus (Outlines of Pyrrhonism, Book III, if I recall correctly); similarly, if you are more familiar with Buddhist philosophy, you can begin with Book I of the Mūlamadhyamakakārikā. EDIT: I should clarify that none of these thinkers suggest that there are events that are uncaused; this is one of the positions that Nāgārjuna explicitly rejects in the first verse of the Mūlamadhyamakakārikā. Rather, each calls into question the notion of causality, and attacks either the notion of "sufficiency" or the notion of "reason" with regard to the matter. A: I'm not sure the idea of "reason" is sufficiently specifiable for the question to make sense. I am not familiar with arguments that events are not caused, but there are at least a couple of reasons to be worried. Since quantum mechanics seems non-deterministic, one could argue that things do indeed happen without reason...or one could broaden the definition of "things" and "reason" so that QM fits nicely within the box. texts.full_text: cost the state business, wasn’t supported by Maryland law enforcement and didn’t make us any safer. Beretta, a gun manufacturer who has been located in Maryland for decades, pays approximately $31 million in taxes and employs 400+ people, is leaving and relocating to a more gun friendly state. Liberal legislators shrugged off the threat of loss of beretta and their enormous tax revenue, and ignored law enforcement. Not to mention that the Columbia Mall shooting showed in no uncertain terms that the law didn’t make anyone safer. The shooter used a shot gun purchased legally under the new legislation. Speaking of tax revenue, remember the Revolutionary War and “no taxation without representation?” Well, when we have legislators who actively ignore a large segment of their constituency and vote in contravention to their interests, we have no representation. Martin O’Malley has instituted 40+ new and increased taxes and fees since he took office in 2007. Those taxes have driven tax paying businesses and wealthier individuals out of our state, cutting revenue, not bolstering it. These increases pushed Maryland into the top 10 of states that tax the highest. The revenues weren’t used as promised, went to heavily Democrat voting areas and didn’t benefit the majority of Marylanders. One of the most important things that we talked about, beyond the why’s and how’s (see my past article for how’s) was “why not just pack up and leave?” Mary asked that of each of us and there are two important reasons why we don’t. First, it doesn’t solve the problem. It just foists it off onto whomever stays.

texts.full_text: Weekly Daf For the week ending 16 July 2005 / 9 Tammuz 5765 Shabbos 72 - 78 by Rabbi Mendel Weinbach zt'l When a Little is Still A Lot In his interpretation of a beraisa concerning the laws of borer (separating the desirable food from the undesirable) on Shabbos, Rabbi Chisda suggests that if one makes such an act of separation on an amount less than the size of a dried fig, he has done nothing wrong. His view is challenged by Rabbi Yosef, who argues that even though one is not obligated to bring a sin-offering if he involuntarily performed borer on less than that amount, it is still forbidden to do so. Rashi explainsthat the latter view reflects an important principle of Jewish law: even though one may not be obligated in a particular mitzvah (commandment), if there are circumstances where performing that action would have positive consequences, it is still considered to refrain from doing so.
